data.narrative:
Mistral AI, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothée Lacroix, is valued at €11.7B ($12.5B equivalent) after raising $14B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

The peer-median method is applicable given sufficient comparables within the AI & Software private sector. The median valuation across these five peers stands at $29.3B, with a range spanning $7.0B (Groq) to $300.0B (OpenAI). This dispersion reflects material variance in market perception across frontier AI firms, driven by differences in model architecture, deployment maturity, and capital deployment.

Triangulation across available methods yields a valuation range of $24.9B (low) to $33.7B (median to high). Mistral's current valuation of €11.7B implies a 60% upside to the median peer benchmark. Key uncertainties include the durability of Mistral's competitive positioning relative to larger-capitalized peers, realized go-to-market traction, and the sustainability of private-market valuations in this cohort given extreme sensitivity to perceived capability deltas and capital availability. The single applicable method limits conviction; additional valuation triangulation via DCF or transaction comparables would strengthen analytical confidence.
